frameworks.name: higher-order thought theory
frameworks.one_line: Mental state consciousness arises when a state is represented by a higher-order thought.

frameworks.name: quality-space theory
frameworks.one_line: Sensory qualities are positions within a structured similarity space of discriminable properties.

frameworks.name: confidence-based metacognition
frameworks.one_line: Subjective confidence is proposed to track the presence of qualitative consciousness.

core_question: Does subjective confidence reliably track the presence of qualitative conscious experience?
key_assumptions: Qualitative consciousness can be decomposed into mental-state consciousness and sensory quality
Sensory qualities have a relational structure expressible as a quality space
Higher-order representations are constitutive of state consciousness
out_of_scope_note: Edited-volume book chapter on Rosenthal's higher-order thought and quality-space theories; theoretical/philosophical rather than empirical, so manipulations and findings are empty.
